\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeActionQuantile() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeActionQuantile}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeActionQuantile}}{\emph{action}, \emph{Debug=True}}{}
renders the overall performance quantile of action

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeAllQuantiles() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeAllQuantiles}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeAllQuantiles}}{\emph{Sorted=True}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
renders a html string showing the table of
the quantiles matrix action x criterion

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeCriterionPerformanceDifferences() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeCriterionPerformanceDifferences}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeCriterionPerformanceDifferences}}{\emph{c}, \emph{Comments=False}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Renders the ordered list of all observed performance differences on the given criterion.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeDefaultDiscriminationThresholds() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeDefaultDiscriminationThresholds}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeDefaultDiscriminationThresholds}}{\emph{criteriaList=None}, \emph{quantile=\{'ind': 10}, \emph{'pref': 20}, \emph{'veto': 80}, \emph{'weakVeto': 60\}}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
updates the discrimination thresholds with the percentiles
from the performance differences.
Parameters: quantile = \{‘ind’: 10, ‘pref’: 20, ‘weakVeto’: 60, ‘veto: 80\}.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeMinMaxEvaluations() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeMinMaxEvaluations}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeMinMaxEvaluations}}{\emph{criteria=None}, \emph{actions=None}}{}
renders minimum and maximum performances on each criterion
in dictionary form: \{‘g’: \{‘minimum’: x, ‘maximum’: x\}\}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeNormalizedDiffEvaluations() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeNormalizedDiffEvaluations}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeNormalizedDiffEvaluations}}{\emph{lowValue=0.0}, \emph{highValue=100.0}, \emph{withOutput=False}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
renders and csv stores (withOutput=True) the
list of normalized evaluation differences observed on the family of criteria
Is only adequate if all criteria have the same
evaluation scale. Therefore the performance tableau is normalized to 0.0-100.0 scales.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computePerformanceDifferences() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computePerformanceDifferences}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computePerformanceDifferences}}{\emph{Comments=False}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{NotPermanentDiffs=True}, \emph{WithMaxMin=False}}{}
Adds to the criteria dictionary the ordered list of all observed performance differences.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeQuantileOrder() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeQuantileOrder}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeQuantileOrder}}{\emph{q0=3}, \emph{q1=0}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=1}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Renders a linear ordering of the decision actions from a simulation of pre-ranked outranking digraphs.

The pre-ranking simulations range by default from
quantiles=q0 to quantiles=min( 100, max(10,len(self.actions)/10{]}) ).

The actions are ordered along a decreasing Borda score of their ranking results.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeQuantilePreorder() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeQuantilePreorder}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeQuantilePreorder}}{\emph{Comments=True}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
computes the preorder of the actions obtained from decreasing majority quantiles. The quantiles are recomputed with a call to the self.computeQuantileSort() method.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeQuantileRanking() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeQuantileRanking}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeQuantileRanking}}{\emph{q0=3}, \emph{q1=0}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=1}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Renders a linear ranking of the decision actions from a simulation of pre-ranked outranking digraphs.

The pre-ranking simulations range by default from
quantiles=q0 to qantiles=min( 100, max(10,len(self.actions)/10) ).

The actions are ordered along an increasing Borda score of their ranking results.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeQuantileSort() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeQuantileSort}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeQuantileSort}}{}{}
shows a sorting of the actions from decreasing majority quantiles

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeQuantiles() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeQuantiles}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeQuantiles}}{\emph{Debug=False}}{}
renders a quantiles matrix action x criterion with the performance quantile of action on criterion

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\index{computeWeightedAveragePerformances() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.computeWeightedAveragePerformances}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeWeightedAveragePerformances}}{\emph{isNormalized=False}, \emph{lowValue=0.0}, \emph{highValue=100.0}, \emph{isListRanked=False}}{}
Compute normalized weighted average scores
Normalization transforms by default all the scores into a
common 0-100 scale. A lowValue and highValue parameter
can be provided for a specific normalisation.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{showHTMLPerformanceHeatmap() (perfTabs.PerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau.showHTMLPerformanceHeatmap}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{showHTMLPerformanceHeatmap}}{\emph{actionsList=None}, \emph{criteriaList=None}, \emph{colorLevels=7}, \emph{pageTitle=None}, \emph{ndigits=2}, \emph{SparseModel=False}, \emph{minimalComponentSize=1}, \emph{rankingRule='Copeland'}, \emph{quantiles=None}, \emph{strategy='average'}, \emph{Correlations=False}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
shows the html heatmap version of the performance tableau in a browser window
(see perfTabs.htmlPerformanceHeatMap() method ).

\sphinxstylestrong{Parameters}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxstyleemphasis{actionsList} and \sphinxstyleemphasis{criteriaList}, if provided,  give the possibility to show the decision alternatives, resp. criteria, in a given ordering.

\item {} 
\sphinxstyleemphasis{ndigits} = 0 may be used to show integer evaluation values.

\item {} 
If no \sphinxstyleemphasis{actionsList} is provided, the decision actions are ordered from the best to the worst. This
ranking is obtained by default with the Copeland rule applied on a standard \sphinxstyleemphasis{BipolarOutrankingDigraph}.
When the \sphinxstyleemphasis{SparseModel} flag is put to \sphinxstyleemphasis{True}, a sparse \sphinxstyleemphasis{PreRankedOutrankingDigraph} construction is used instead.

\item {} 
The \sphinxstyleemphasis{minimalComponentSize} allows to control the fill rate of the pre-ranked model.
If \sphinxstyleemphasis{minimalComponentSize} = \sphinxstyleemphasis{n} (the number of decision actions) both the pre-ranked model will be
in fact equivalent to the standard model.

\item {} 
It may interesting in some cases to use \sphinxstyleemphasis{rankingRule} = ‘NetFlows’.

\item {} 
Quantiles used for the pre-ranked decomposition are put by default to \sphinxstyleemphasis{n}
(the number of decision alternatives) for \sphinxstyleemphasis{n} \textless{} 50. For larger cardinalities up to 1000, quantiles = \sphinxstyleemphasis{n} /10.
For bigger performance tableaux the \sphinxstyleemphasis{quantiles} parameter may be set to a much lower value
not exceeding usually 1000.

\item {} 
The pre-ranking may be obtained with three ordering strategies for the
quantiles equivalence classes: ‘average’ (default), ‘optimistic’ or  ‘pessimistic’.

\item {} 
With \sphinxstyleemphasis{Correlations} = \sphinxstyleemphasis{True} and \sphinxstyleemphasis{criteriaList} = \sphinxstyleemphasis{None}, the criteria will be presented from left to right in decreasing
order of the correlations between the marginal criterion based ranking and the global ranking used for
presenting the decision alternatives.

\item {} 
For large performance Tableaux, \sphinxstyleemphasis{multiprocessing} techniques may be used by setting
\sphinxstyleemphasis{Threading} = \sphinxstyleemphasis{True} in order to speed up the computations; especially when \sphinxstyleemphasis{Correlations} = \sphinxstyleemphasis{True}.

\item {} 
By default, the number of cores available, will be detected. It may be efficient in a HPC context
to indicate the exact number of singled threaded cores in fact allocated to the job.

\end{itemize}

\fvset{hllines={, ,}}%
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{randomPerfTabs} \PYG{k}{import} \PYG{n}{RandomPerformanceTableau}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{rt} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RandomPerformanceTableau}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{seed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{rt}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{showHTMLPerformanceHeatmap}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{colorLevels}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{Correlations}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\noindent{\hspace*{\fill}\sphinxincludegraphics[width=600\sphinxpxdimen]{{perfTabsExample}.png}\hspace*{\fill}}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{XMCDA2PerformanceTableau (class in perfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.XMCDA2PerformanceTableau}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{perfTabs.}\sphinxbfcode{XMCDA2PerformanceTableau}}{\emph{fileName='temp'}, \emph{HasSeparatedWeights=False}, \emph{HasSeparatedThresholds=False}, \emph{stringInput=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Specialization of the general PerformanceTableau class for reading
stored XMCDA 2.0 formatted instances with exact decimal numbers.
Using the inbuilt module xml.etree (for Python 2.5+).
\begin{description}
\item[{Parameters:}] \leavevmode\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
fileName is given without the extension \sphinxcode{.xml} or \sphinxcode{.xmcda},

\item {} 
HasSeparatedWeights in XMCDA 2.0.0 encoding (default = False),

\item {} 
HasSeparatedThresholds in XMCDA 2.0.0 encoding (default = False),

\item {} 
stringInput: instantiates from an XMCDA 2.0 encoded string argument.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\label{\detokenize{techDoc:performancequantiles-module}}\label{\detokenize{techDoc:performancequantiles-label}}\label{\detokenize{techDoc:module-performanceQuantiles}}\index{performanceQuantiles (module)}
Digraph3 collection of python3 modules for Algorithmic Decision Theory applications

Module for incremental performance quantiles computation

\index{PerformanceQuantiles (class in performanceQuantiles)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:performanceQuantiles.PerformanceQuantiles}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{performanceQuantiles.}\sphinxbfcode{PerformanceQuantiles}}{\emph{perfTab=None}, \emph{numberOfBins=4}, \emph{LowerClosed=True}, \emph{filePerfQuant=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Implements the incremental performance quantiles representation of a
given performance tableau.

\sphinxstyleemphasis{NumberOfBins} may be either ‘quartiles’, ‘deciles’, … or ‘n’, the integer number of bins.
\begin{description}
\item[{Example python session:}] \leavevmode
\fvset{hllines={, ,}}%
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{performanceQuantiles}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{randomPerfTabs} \PYG{k}{import} \PYG{n}{RandomCBPerformanceTableau}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{randomPerfTabs} \PYG{k}{import} \PYG{n}{RandomCBPerformanceGenerator} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n}{PerfTabGenerator}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{nbrActions}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1000}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{nbrCrit} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mi}{7}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{tp} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RandomCBPerformanceTableau}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{numberOfActions}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{nbrActions}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }                               \PYG{n}{numberOfCriteria}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{nbrCrit}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{seed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{105}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pq} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{performanceQuantiles}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{PerformanceQuantiles}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{tp}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{quintiles}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }                               \PYG{n}{LowerClosed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{Debug}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{False}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pq}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{showLimitingQuantiles}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ByObjectives}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{*\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}  performance quantiles \PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}*}
\PYG{g+go}{Costs}
\PYG{g+go}{criteria  \textbar{} weights \textbar{}  \PYGZsq{}0.0\PYGZsq{}   \PYGZsq{}0.25\PYGZsq{}  \PYGZsq{}0.5\PYGZsq{}   \PYGZsq{}0.75\PYGZsq{}  \PYGZsq{}1.0\PYGZsq{}   }
\PYG{g+go}{ \PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\textbar{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}}
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZsq{}c1\PYGZsq{}  \textbar{}   6     \textbar{} \PYGZhy{}97.12  \PYGZhy{}65.70  \PYGZhy{}46.08  \PYGZhy{}24.96   \PYGZhy{}1.85  }
\PYG{g+go}{Benefits}
\PYG{g+go}{criteria  \textbar{} weights \textbar{}  \PYGZsq{}0.0\PYGZsq{}   \PYGZsq{}0.25\PYGZsq{}  \PYGZsq{}0.5\PYGZsq{}   \PYGZsq{}0.75\PYGZsq{}  \PYGZsq{}1.0\PYGZsq{}   }
\PYG{g+go}{ \PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\textbar{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}}
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZsq{}b1\PYGZsq{}  \textbar{}   1     \textbar{}   2.11   32.42   53.25   73.44   98.69 }
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZsq{}b2\PYGZsq{}  \textbar{}   1     \textbar{}   0.00    3.00    5.00    7.00   10.00  }
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZsq{}b3\PYGZsq{}  \textbar{}   1     \textbar{}   1.08   34.64   54.80   73.24   97.23  }
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZsq{}b4\PYGZsq{}  \textbar{}   1     \textbar{}   0.00    3.00    5.00    7.00   10.00  }
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZsq{}b5\PYGZsq{}  \textbar{}   1     \textbar{}   1.84   34.25   55.11   74.62   96.40  }
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZsq{}b6\PYGZsq{}  \textbar{}   1     \textbar{}   0.00    3.00    5.00    7.00   10.00  }
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{tpg} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PerfTabGenerator}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{tp}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{seed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{105}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{newActions} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{tpg}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{randomActions}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pq}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{updateQuantiles}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{newActions}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{historySize}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{)}      
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pq}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{showHTMLLimitingQuantiles}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{Transposed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{description}

\index{updateQuantiles() (performanceQuantiles.PerformanceQuantiles method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:performanceQuantiles.PerformanceQuantiles.updateQuantiles}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{updateQuantiles}}{\emph{newData}, \emph{historySize=None}}{}
Update the PerformanceQuantiles with a set of new random decision actions.
Parameter \sphinxstyleemphasis{historysize} allows to take more or less into account the historical situtaion.
For instance, \sphinxstyleemphasis{historySize=0} does not take into account at all any past observations.
Otherwise, if \sphinxstyleemphasis{historySize=None} (the default setting), the new observations become less and less
influential compared to the historical data.

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\subsection{randomPerfTabs module}
\label{\detokenize{techDoc:randomperftabs-module}}\label{\detokenize{techDoc:randomperftabs-label}}
A tutorial with coding examples is available here: {\hyperref[\detokenize{tutorial:randomperformancetableau-tutorial-label}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{std,std-ref}{Generating random performance tableaux}}}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:module-randomPerfTabs}}\index{randomPerfTabs (module)}\index{Random3ObjectivesPerformanceGenerator (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.Random3ObjectivesPerformanceGenerator}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{randomPerfTabs.}\sphinxbfcode{Random3ObjectivesPerformanceGenerator}}{\emph{argPerfTab}, \emph{actionNamePrefix='a'}, \emph{instanceCounter=0}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomPerformanceGenerator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{randomPerfTabs.RandomPerformanceGenerator}}}}

Generates and/or new decision actions with random evaluation for a
given Random3ObjectivesPerformanceTableau instance.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{Random3ObjectivesPerformanceTableau (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.Random3ObjectivesPerformanceTableau}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{randomPerfTabs.}\sphinxbfcode{Random3ObjectivesPerformanceTableau}}{\emph{numberOfActions=20}, \emph{numberOfCriteria=13}, \emph{weightDistribution='equiobjectives'}, \emph{weightScale=None}, \emph{IntegerWeights=True}, \emph{OrdinalScales=False}, \emph{commonScale=None}, \emph{commonThresholds=None}, \emph{commonMode=None}, \emph{valueDigits=2}, \emph{vetoProbability=0.5}, \emph{missingDataProbability=0.05}, \emph{BigData=False}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Specialization of the PerformanceTableau
for 3 objectives: \sphinxstyleemphasis{Eco}, \sphinxstyleemphasis{Soc} and \sphinxstyleemphasis{Env}.

Each decision action is qualified at random as weak (-), fair (\textasciitilde{}) or good (+)
on each of the three objectives.
\begin{description}
\item[{Generator arguments:}] \leavevmode\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
numberOf Actions := 20 (default)

\item {} 
number of Criteria := 13 (default)

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{weightDistribution := ‘equiobjectives’ (default)}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] ‘equisignificant’ (weights set all to 1)
\item[] ‘random’ (in the range 1 to numberOfCriteria)
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\item {} 
weightScale := {[}1,numerOfCriteria{]} (random default)

\item {} 
IntegerWeights := True (default) / False

\item {} 
OrdinalScales := True / False (default), if True commonScale is set to (0,10)

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{commonScale := (Min, Max)}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] when common Scale = False, (0.0,10.0) by default if OrdinalScales == True and CommonScale=None,
\item[] and (0.0,100.0) by default otherwise 
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{commonThresholds := ((Ind,Ind\_slope),(Pref,Pref\_slope),(Veto,Veto\_slope)) with}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] Ind \textless{} Pref \textless{} Veto in {[}0.0,100.0{]} such that 
\item[] (Ind/100.0*span + Ind\_slope*x) \textless{} (Pref/100.0*span + Pref\_slope*x) \textless{} (Pref/100.0*span + Pref\_slope*x)
\item[] By default {[}(0.05*span,0.0),(0.10*span,0.0),(0.60*span,0.0){]} if OrdinalScales=False
\item[] By default {[}(0.1*span,0.0),(0.2*span,0.0),(0.8*span,0.0){]} otherwise
\item[] with span = commonScale{[}1{]} - commonScale{[}0{]}.
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{commonMode := {[}‘triangular’,’variable’,0.50{]} (default), A constant mode may be provided.}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] {[}‘uniform’,’variable’,None{]}, a constant range may be provided.
\item[] {[}‘beta’,’variable’,None{]} (three alpha, beta combinations:
\item[] (5.8661,2.62203),(5.05556,5.05556) and (2.62203, 5.8661)
\item[] chosen by default for ‘good’, ‘fair’ and ‘weak’ evaluations. 
\item[] Constant parameters may be provided.
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\item {} 
valueDigits := 2 (default, for cardinal scales only)

\item {} 
vetoProbability := x in {]}0.0-1.0{[} (0.5 default), probability that a cardinal criterion shows a veto preference discrimination threshold.

\item {} 
Debug := True / False (default)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\index{RandomCBPerformanceGenerator (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomCBPerformanceGenerator}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{randomPerfTabs.}\sphinxbfcode{RandomCBPerformanceGenerator}}{\emph{argPerfTab}, \emph{actionNamePrefix='a'}, \emph{instanceCounter=None}, \emph{seed=None}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomPerformanceGenerator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{randomPerfTabs.RandomPerformanceGenerator}}}}

Instantiates a generator of new decision actions with associated random evaluations using the model parameters provided by a given RandomCBPerformanceTableau instance.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{RandomCBPerformanceTableau (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomCBPerformanceTableau}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{randomPerfTabs.}\sphinxbfcode{RandomCBPerformanceTableau}}{\emph{numberOfActions=13}, \emph{numberOfCriteria=7}, \emph{name='randomCBperftab'}, \emph{weightDistribution='equiobjectives'}, \emph{weightScale=None}, \emph{IntegerWeights=True}, \emph{NegativeWeights=False}, \emph{commonScale=None}, \emph{commonThresholds=None}, \emph{commonPercentiles=None}, \emph{samplingSize=100000}, \emph{commonMode=None}, \emph{valueDigits=2}, \emph{missingDataProbability=0.01}, \emph{BigData=False}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrCores=None}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Full automatic generation of random
Cost versus Benefit oriented performance tableaux.

Parameters:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
If numberOfActions == None, a uniform random number between 10 and 31 of cheap, neutral or advantageous actions (equal 1/3 probability each type) actions is instantiated

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{If numberOfCriteria == None, a uniform random number between 5 and 21 of cost or benefit criteria. }] \leavevmode
Cost criteria have probability 1/3, whereas benefit criteria respectively 2/3 probability to be generated.
However, at least one criterion of each kind is always instantiated.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{weightDistribution := \{‘equiobjectives’\textbar{}’fixed’\textbar{}’random’\textbar{}’equisignificant’\} By default, the sum of}] \leavevmode
significance of the cost criteria is set equal to the sum of the significance of the benefit criteria.

\end{description}

\item {} 
default weightScale for ‘random’ weightDistribution is 1 - numberOfCriteria.

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{commonScale parameter is not used. The scale of cost criteria is cardinal or ordinal (0-10)}] \leavevmode
with proabailities 1/4 respectively 3/4, whereas the scale of benefit criteria is ordinal or cardinal
with probabilities 2/3, respectively 1/3.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{All cardinal criteria are evaluated with decimals between 0.0 and 100.0 wheras all ordinal criteria}] \leavevmode
are evaluated with integers between 0 and 10.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{commonThresholds parameter is not used. Preference discrimination is specified as percentiles of}] \leavevmode
concerned performance differences (see below).

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{CommonPercentiles = \{‘ind’:0.05, ‘pref’:0.10, ‘veto’:‘95\} are expressed}] \leavevmode
in percentiles of the observed performance differences and only concern cardinal criteria.

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}
Minimal number of decision actions required is 3 !
\end{sphinxadmonition}
\index{updateDiscriminationThresholds() (randomPerfTabs.RandomCBPerformanceTableau method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomCBPerformanceTableau.updateDiscriminationThresholds}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{updateDiscriminationThresholds}}{\emph{Comments=False}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{RandomPerformanceTableau (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomPerformanceTableau}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{randomPerfTabs.}\sphinxbfcode{RandomPerformanceTableau}}{\emph{numberOfActions=13}, \emph{actionNamePrefix='a'}, \emph{numberOfCriteria=7}, \emph{weightDistribution='equisignificant'}, \emph{weightScale=None}, \emph{IntegerWeights=True}, \emph{commonScale=(0.0}, \emph{100.0)}, \emph{commonThresholds=((2.5}, \emph{0.0)}, \emph{(5.0}, \emph{0.0)}, \emph{(80.0}, \emph{0.0))}, \emph{commonMode=('beta'}, \emph{None}, \emph{(2}, \emph{2))}, \emph{valueDigits=2}, \emph{missingDataProbability=0.025}, \emph{BigData=False}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Specialization of the generic \sphinxcode{perftabs.PerformanceTableau} class for generating a temporary random performance tableau.
\begin{description}
\item[{Parameters:}] \leavevmode\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
numberOfActions := nbr of decision actions.

\item {} 
numberOfCriteria := number performance criteria.

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{weightDistribution := ‘random’ (default) \textbar{} ‘fixed’ \textbar{} ‘equisignificant’.}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] If ‘random’, weights are uniformly selected randomly
\item[] form the given weight scale;
\item[] If ‘fixed’, the weightScale must provided a corresponding weights
\item[] distribution;
\item[] If ‘equisignificant’, all criterion weights are put to unity.
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\item {} 
weightScale := {[}Min,Max{]} (default ={[}1,numberOfCriteria{]}.

\item {} 
IntegerWeights := True (default) \textbar{} False (normalized to proportions of 1.0).

\item {} 
commonScale := {[}Min;Max{]}; common performance measuring scales (default = {[}0;100{]})

\item {} 
commonThresholds := {[}(q0,q1),(p0,p1),(v0,v1){]}; common indifference(q), preference (p)
and considerable performance difference discrimination thresholds. q0, p0 and v0 are
expressed in percentige of the common scale amplitude: Max - Min.

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{commonMode := common random distribution of random performance measurements:}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] (‘uniform’,None,None), uniformly distributed between min and max values. 
\item[] (‘normal’,mu,sigma), truncated Gaussion distribution. 
\item[] (‘triangular’,mode,repartition), generalized triangular distribution 
\item[] (‘beta’,mod,(alpha,beta)), mode in {]}0,1{[}.
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\item {} 
valueDigits := \textless{}integer\textgreater{}, precision of performance measurements
(2 decimal digits by default).

\end{itemize}

\item[{Code example:}] \leavevmode
\fvset{hllines={, ,}}%
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{randomPerfTabs} \PYG{k}{import} \PYG{n}{RandomPerformanceTableau}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RandomPerformanceTableau}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{numberOfActions}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{3}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{numberOfCriteria}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{seed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{actions}
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}a1\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}comment\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}RandomPerformanceTableau() generated.\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}name\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}random decision action\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{},}
\PYG{g+go}{     \PYGZsq{}a2\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}comment\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}RandomPerformanceTableau() generated.\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}name\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}random decision action\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{},}
\PYG{g+go}{     \PYGZsq{}a3\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}comment\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}RandomPerformanceTableau() generated.\PYGZsq{}, \PYGZsq{}name\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}random decision action\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{criteria}
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}g1\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}thresholds\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}ind\PYGZsq{} : (Decimal(\PYGZsq{}10.0\PYGZsq{}), Decimal(\PYGZsq{}0.0\PYGZsq{})),}
\PYG{g+go}{                           \PYGZsq{}veto\PYGZsq{}: (Decimal(\PYGZsq{}80.0\PYGZsq{}), Decimal(\PYGZsq{}0.0\PYGZsq{})),}
\PYG{g+go}{                           \PYGZsq{}pref\PYGZsq{}: (Decimal(\PYGZsq{}20.0\PYGZsq{}), Decimal(\PYGZsq{}0.0\PYGZsq{}))\PYGZcb{},}
\PYG{g+go}{            \PYGZsq{}scale\PYGZsq{}: [0.0, 100.0],}
\PYG{g+go}{            \PYGZsq{}weight\PYGZsq{}: Decimal(\PYGZsq{}1\PYGZsq{}),}
\PYG{g+go}{            \PYGZsq{}name\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}digraphs.RandomPerformanceTableau() instance\PYGZsq{},}
\PYG{g+go}{            \PYGZsq{}comment\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZsq{}Arguments: ; weightDistribution=random;}
\PYG{g+go}{                weightScale=(1, 1); commonMode=None\PYGZsq{}\PYGZcb{}\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{evaluation}
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}g01\PYGZsq{}: \PYGZob{}\PYGZsq{}a01\PYGZsq{}: Decimal(\PYGZsq{}45.95\PYGZsq{}),}
\PYG{g+go}{             \PYGZsq{}a02\PYGZsq{}: Decimal(\PYGZsq{}95.17\PYGZsq{}),}
\PYG{g+go}{             \PYGZsq{}a03\PYGZsq{}: Decimal(\PYGZsq{}17.47\PYGZsq{})}
\PYG{g+go}{            \PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{g+go}{    \PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{RandomRankPerformanceTableau (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomRankPerformanceTableau}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{randomPerfTabs.}\sphinxbfcode{RandomRankPerformanceTableau}}{\emph{numberOfActions=13}, \emph{numberOfCriteria=7}, \emph{weightDistribution='equisignificant'}, \emph{weightScale=None}, \emph{commonThresholds=None}, \emph{IntegerWeights=True}, \emph{BigData=False}, \emph{seed=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Specialization of the PerformanceTableau class for generating a temporary
random performance tableau.

Random generator for multiple criteria ranked (without ties) performances of a
given number of decision actions. On each criterion,
all decision actions are hence lineraly ordered. The RandomRankPerformanceTableau class is
matching the RandomLinearVotingProfiles class (see the votingDigraphs module)
\begin{description}
\item[{\sphinxstyleemphasis{Parameters}:}] \leavevmode\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
number of actions,

\item {} 
number of performance criteria,

\item {} 
weightDistribution := equisignificant \textbar{} random (default, see RandomPerformanceTableau)

\item {} 
weightScale := (1, 1 \textbar{} numberOfCriteria (default when random))

\item {} 
IntegerWeights := Boolean (True = default)

\item {} \begin{description}
\item[{commonThresholds (default) := \{}] \leavevmode
\begin{DUlineblock}{0em}
\item[] ‘ind’:(0,0),
\item[] ‘pref’:(1,0),
\item[] ‘veto’:(numberOfActions,0)
\item[] \} (default)
\end{DUlineblock}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{RandomStdPerformanceGenerator (class in randomPerfTabs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:randomPerfTabs.RandomStdPerformanceGenerator}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{randomPerfTabs.}\sphinxbfcode{RandomStdPerformanceGenerator}}{\emph{argPerfTab}, \emph{actionNamePrefix='a'}, \emph{instanceCounter=0}, \emph{seed=None}}{}

Generates for a given standard RandomPerformanceTableau instance.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{BipolarOutrankingDigraph (class in outrankingDigraphs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.BipolarOutrankingDigraph}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{outrankingDigraphs.}\sphinxbfcode{BipolarOutrankingDigraph}}{\emph{argPerfTab=None}, \emph{coalition=None}, \emph{actionsSubset=None}, \emph{hasNoVeto=False}, \emph{hasBipolarVeto=True}, \emph{Normalized=False}, \emph{CopyPerfTab=True}, \emph{BigData=False}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{tempDir=None}, \emph{WithConcordanceRelation=True}, \emph{WithVetoCounts=True}, \emph{nbrCores=None}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.OutrankingDigraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{outrankingDigraphs.OutrankingDigraph}}}}

Specialization of the abstract OutrankingDigraph root class for generating
bipolarly-valued outranking digraphs.
\begin{description}
\item[{Parameters:}] \leavevmode\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
argPerfTab: instance of PerformanceTableau class.
If a file name string is given, the performance tableau will directly be loaded first.

\item {} 
coalition: subset of criteria to be used for contructing the outranking digraph.

\item {} 
hasNoVeto: veto desactivation flag (False by default).

\item {} 
hasBipolarVeto: bipolar versus electre veto activation (true by default).

\item {} 
Normalized: the valuation domain is set by default to {[}-100,+100{]} (bipolar percents).
If True, the valuation domain is recoded to {[}-1.0,+1.0{]}.

\item {} 
WithConcordanceRelation: True by default when not threading.
The self.concordanceRelation contains the significance majority margin of the “at least as good relation as”
without the large performance difference polarization.

\item {} 
WithVetoCounts: True by default when not threading. All vetos and countervetos
are stored in self.vetos and self.negativeVetos slots,
as well the counts of large performance differences in self.largePerformanceDifferencesCount slot.

\item {} 
Threading: False by default. Allows to profit from SMP machines via the Python multiprocessing module.

\item {} 
nbrCores: controls the maximal number of cores that will be used in the multiprocessing phases.
If None is given, the os.cpu\_count method is used in order to determine the number of availble cores on the SMP machine.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}
If Threading is True, WithConcordanceRelation and WithVetoCounts flags are automatically set both to False.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\index{ConfidentBipolarOutrankingDigraph (class in outrankingDigraphs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.ConfidentBipolarOutrankingDigraph}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{outrankingDigraphs.}\sphinxbfcode{ConfidentBipolarOutrankingDigraph}}{\emph{argPerfTab=None}, \emph{distribution='triangular'}, \emph{betaParameter=2}, \emph{confidence=90.0}, \emph{coalition=None}, \emph{hasNoVeto=False}, \emph{hasBipolarVeto=True}, \emph{Normalized=True}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=1}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.BipolarOutrankingDigraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{outrankingDigraphs.BipolarOutrankingDigraph}}}}

Confident bipolar outranking digraph based on multiple criteria of
uncertain significance.

The digraph’s bipolar valuation represents the bipolar outranking relation
based on a sufficient likelihood of the at least as good as relation
that is outranking without veto and counterveto.

By default, each criterion i’ significance weight is supposed to
be a triangular random variable of mode w\_i in the range 0 to 2*w\_i.

\sphinxstyleemphasis{Parameters}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
argPerfTab: PerformanceTableau instance or the name (without extension) of a stored one. If None, a random instance is generated.

\item {} 
distribution: \{triangular\textbar{}uniform\textbar{}beta\}, probability distribution used for generating random weights

\item {} 
betaParameter: a = b (default = 2)

\item {} 
confidence: required likelihood (in \%) of the outranking relation

\item {} 
other standard parameters from the BipolarOutrankingDigraph class (see documentation).

\end{itemize}

\index{OutrankingDigraph (class in outrankingDigraphs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.OutrankingDigraph}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{outrankingDigraphs.}\sphinxbfcode{OutrankingDigraph}}{\emph{file=None}, \emph{order=7}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:digraphs.Digraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{digraphs.Digraph}}}}, {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Abstract root class for \sphinxstylestrong{outranking digraphs} inheritating methods both from the generic {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:digraphs.Digraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{digraphs.Digraph}}}}
and from the generic {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}} root classes.
As such, our genuine outranking digraph model is a hybrid object appearing on the one side as digraph with a
nodes set (the decision alternatives) and a binary relation (outranking situations) and, on the other side, as
a performance tableau with a set of decision alternatives, performance criteria and a table of performance measurements.

Provides common methods to all specialized models of outranking digraphs, the standard outranking digraph model being provided
by the {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.BipolarOutrankingDigraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{outrankingDigraphs.BipolarOutrankingDigraph}}}} class.

A given object of this class consists at least in:
\begin{enumerate}
\item {} 
a potential set of decision \sphinxstylestrong{actions} : an (ordered) dictionary describing the potential decision actions or alternatives with ‘name’ and ‘comment’ attributes,

\item {} 
a coherent family of \sphinxstylestrong{criteria}: an (ordered) dictionary of criteria functions used for measuring the performance of each potential decision action with respect to the preference dimension captured by each criterion,

\item {} 
the \sphinxstylestrong{evaluations}: a dictionary of performance evaluations for each decision action or alternative on each criterion function.

\item {} 
the digraph \sphinxstylestrong{valuationdomain}, a dictionary with three entries: the \sphinxstyleemphasis{minimum} (-100, means certainly no link), the \sphinxstyleemphasis{median} (0, means missing information) and the \sphinxstyleemphasis{maximum} characteristic value (+100, means certainly a link),

\item {} 
the \sphinxstylestrong{outranking relation} : a double dictionary defined on the Cartesian product of the set of decision alternatives capturing the credibility of the pairwise \sphinxstyleemphasis{outranking situation} computed on the basis of the performance differences observed between couples of decision alternatives on the given family if criteria functions.

\end{enumerate}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}
Cannot be called directly ! No \_\_init\_\_(self,…) method defined.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\index{computeCriterionCorrelation() (outrankingDigraphs.OutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.OutrankingDigraph.computeCriterionCorrelation}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeCriterionCorrelation}}{\emph{criterion}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=None}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Renders the ordinal correlation coefficient between
the global outranking and the marginal criterion relation.

Uses the digraphs.computeOrdinalCorrelationMP().

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
Renders a dictionary with the key ‘correlation’ containing the actual bipolar correlation index and the key ‘determination’ containing the minimal determination level D of the self outranking and the marginal criterion relation.

D = sum\_\{x != y\} min(abs(self.relation(x,y)),abs(marginalCriterionRelation(x,y)) / n(n-1)

where n is the number of actions considered.

The correlation index with a completely indeterminate relation
is by convention 0.0 at determination level 0.0 .
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.OutrankingDigraph.computeCriterionRelation}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeCriterionRelation}}{\emph{c}, \emph{a}, \emph{b}}{}
compute the outranking characteristic for actions x and y
on criterion c.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeMarginalCorrelation() (outrankingDigraphs.OutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.OutrankingDigraph.computeMarginalCorrelation}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeMarginalCorrelation}}{\emph{args}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=None}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}

given normalized outranking relation.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeMarginalVersusGlobalOutrankingCorrelations() (outrankingDigraphs.OutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.OutrankingDigraph.computeMarginalVersusGlobalOutrankingCorrelations}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeMarginalVersusGlobalOutrankingCorrelations}}{\emph{Sorted=True}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrCores=None}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Method for computing correlations between each individual criterion relation with the corresponding
global outranking relation.

Returns a list of tuples (correlation,criterionKey) sorted by default in decreasing order of the correlation.

If Threading is True, a multiprocessing Pool class is used with a parallel equivalent of the built-in map function.

If nbrCores is not set, the os.cpu\_count() function is used to determine the number of
available cores.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeMarginalVersusGlobalRankingCorrelations() (outrankingDigraphs.OutrankingDigraph method)}

Method for computing correlations between each individual criterion relation with the corresponding
global outranking relation.

Returns a list of tuples (correlation,criterionKey) sorted by default in decreasing order of the correlation.

If Threading is True, a multiprocessing Pool class is used with a parallel equivalent of the built-in map function.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{RubisRestServer (class in outrankingDigraphs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.RubisRestServer}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{outrankingDigraphs.}\sphinxbfcode{RubisRestServer}}{\emph{host='http://leopold-loewenheim.uni.lu/cgi-bin/xmlrpc\_cgi.py'}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3.7/library/xmlrpc.client.html\#xmlrpc.client.ServerProxy}{\sphinxcode{xmlrpc.client.ServerProxy}}

xmlrpc-cgi Proxy Server for accessing on-line
a Rubis Rest Solver.

Example Python3 session:

\fvset{hllines={, ,}}%
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{outrankingDigraphs} \PYG{k}{import} \PYG{n}{RubisRestServer}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{solver} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RubisRestServer}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{solver}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{ping}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{*************************************************}
\PYG{g+go}{* This is the Leopold\PYGZhy{}Loewenheim Apache Server  *}
\PYG{g+go}{* of the University of Luxembourg.              *}
\PYG{g+go}{* Welcome to the Rubis XMCDA 2.0 Web service    *}
\PYG{g+go}{* R. Bisdorff (c) 2009\PYGZhy{}2013                     *}
\PYG{g+go}{* November 2013, version REST/D4 1.1            *}
\PYG{g+go}{*************************************************}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{perfTabs} \PYG{k}{import} \PYG{n}{RandomCBPerformanceTableau}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RandomCBPerformanceTableau}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{numberOfActions}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{numberOfCriteria}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{7}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{solver}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{submitProblem}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{The problem submission was successful !}
\PYG{g+go}{Server ticket: l4qfAP0RfBBvyjsL}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{solver}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{showHTMLSolution}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{Created new window in existing browser session.}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{solver}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{saveXMCDA2Solution}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{The solution request was successful.}
\PYG{g+go}{Saving XMCDA 2.0 encoded solution in file Solutionl4qfAP0RfBBvyjsL.xml}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{o}{.}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\index{StochasticBipolarOutrankingDigraph (class in outrankingDigraphs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.StochasticBipolarOutrankingDigraph}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{outrankingDigraphs.}\sphinxbfcode{StochasticBipolarOutrankingDigraph}}{\emph{argPerfTab=None}, \emph{sampleSize=50}, \emph{samplingSeed=None}, \emph{distribution='triangular'}, \emph{spread=1.0}, \emph{likelihood=0.9}, \emph{coalition=None}, \emph{hasNoVeto=False}, \emph{hasBipolarVeto=True}, \emph{Normalized=False}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{SeeSampleCounter=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.BipolarOutrankingDigraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{outrankingDigraphs.BipolarOutrankingDigraph}}}}

Stochastic bipolar outranking digraph based on multiple criteria of uncertain significance.

The digraph’s bipolar valuation represents the median of sampled outranking relations with a
sufficient likelihood (default = 90\%) to remain positive, repectively negative,
over the possible criteria significance ranges.

Each criterion i’ significance weight is supposed to
be a triangular random variables of mode w\_i in the range 0 to 2*w\_i.

\sphinxstyleemphasis{Parameters}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
argPerfTab: PerformanceTableau instance or the name of a stored one.
If None, a random instance is generated.

\item {} 
sampleSize: number of random weight vectors used for Monte Carlo simulation.

\item {} 
distribution: \{triangular\textbar{}extTriangular\textbar{}uniform\textbar{}beta(2,2)\textbar{}beta(4,4)\}, probability distribution used for generating random weights

\item {} 
spread: weight range = weight mode +- (weight mode * spread)

\item {} 
likelihood: 1.0 - frequency of valuations of opposite sign compared to the median valuation.

\item {} 
other standard parameters from the BipolarOutrankingDigraph class (see documentation).

\end{itemize}
\index{computeCDF() (outrankingDigraphs.StochasticBipolarOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.StochasticBipolarOutrankingDigraph.computeCDF}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeCDF}}{\emph{x}, \emph{y}, \emph{rValue}}{}
computes by interpolation the likelihood of a given rValue with respect to the sampled r(x,y) valuations.

\sphinxstyleemphasis{Parameters}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
action key x

\item {} 
action key y

\item {} 
r(x,y)

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\subsection{xmcda module}
\label{\detokenize{techDoc:module-xmcda}}\label{\detokenize{techDoc:xmcda-module}}\label{\detokenize{techDoc:xmcda-label}}\index{xmcda (module)}\index{saveRobustRubisChoiceXSL() (in module xmcda)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:xmcda.saveRobustRubisChoiceXSL}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{xmcda.}\sphinxbfcode{saveRobustRubisChoiceXSL}}{\emph{fileName='xmcda2RubisRobustChoice.xsl'}}{}
Save the robust version of the Rubis Best-Choice XMCDA 2.0 XSL style sheet in the current working directory
This style sheet allows to browse an XMCDA 2.0 encoded robust Rubis Best-Choice recommendation.

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
When accessing an XMCDA encoded data file in a browser, for safety reasons, the corresponding XSL style sheet
must be present in the same working directory.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{saveRubisChoiceXSL() (in module xmcda)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:xmcda.saveRubisChoiceXSL}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{xmcda.}\sphinxbfcode{saveRubisChoiceXSL}}{\emph{fileName='xmcda2RubisChoice.xsl'}}{}
Save the local Rubis Best-Choice XMCDA 2.0 XSL style sheet in the current working directory
This style sheet allows to browse an XMCDA 2.0 encoded Rubis Best-Choice recommendation.

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
When accessing an XMCDA encoded data file in a browser, for safety reasons, the corresponding XSL style sheet
must be present in the same working directory.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{saveRubisXSL() (in module xmcda)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:xmcda.saveRubisXSL}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{xmcda.}\sphinxbfcode{saveRubisXSL}}{\emph{fileName='xmcda2Rubis.xsl'}, \emph{Extended=True}}{}
Save the standard Rubis XMCDA 2.0 XSL style sheet in the current working directory.
This style sheet allows to visualize an XMCDA 2.0 encoded performance tableau in a browser.

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
When accessing an XMCDA encoded data file in a browser, for safety reasons, the corresponding XSL style sheet
must be present in the same working directory.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{saveXMCDARubisBestChoiceRecommendation() (in module xmcda)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:xmcda.saveXMCDARubisBestChoiceRecommendation}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{xmcda.}\sphinxbfcode{saveXMCDARubisBestChoiceRecommendation}}{\emph{problemFileName=None}, \emph{tempDir='.'}, \emph{valuationType=None}}{}
Store an XMCDA2 encoded solution file of the Rubis best-choice recommendation.
\begin{quote}

The valuationType parameter allows to work:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
on the standard bipolar outranking digraph (valuationType = ‘bipolar’, default),

\item {} 
on the normalized \textendash{}{[}-1,1{]} valued\textendash{} bipolar outranking digraph (valuationType = ‘normalized’),

\item {} 
on the robust \textendash{}ordinal criteria weights\textendash{} bipolar outranking digraph (valuationType = ‘robust’),

\item {} 
on the confident outranking digraph (valuationType = ‘confident’),

\item {} 
ignoring considerable performances differences (valuationType = ‘noVeto’).

\end{itemize}
\end{quote}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
The method requires an Unix like OS like Ubuntu or Mac OSX and depends on:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
the R statistics package for Principal Component Analysis graphic,

\item {} 
the C calmat matrix interpreter (On \sphinxurl{http://leopold-loewenheim.uni.lu/svn/repos/Calmat/}  see README)

\item {} 
the xpdf ressources ( …\$ apt-get install xpdf on Ubuntu) for converting pdf files to ppm format, and ppm files to png format.

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{showXMCDARubisBestChoiceRecommendation() (in module xmcda)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:xmcda.showXMCDARubisBestChoiceRecommendation}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxcode{xmcda.}\sphinxbfcode{showXMCDARubisBestChoiceRecommendation}}{\emph{problemFileName=None}, \emph{valuationType=None}}{}
Launches a browser window with the XMCDA2 solution of the
Rubis Solver computed from a stored XMCDA2 encoded performance tableau.

The valuationType parameter allows to work:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
on the standard bipolar outranking digraph (valuationType = ‘bipolar’, default),

\item {} 
on the normalized \textendash{}{[}-1,1{]} valued\textendash{} bipolar outranking digraph (valuationType = ‘normalized’),

\item {} 
on the robust \textendash{}ordinal criteria weights\textendash{} bipolar outranking digraph (valuationType = ‘robust’),

\item {} 
on the confident outranking digraph (valuationType = ‘confident’),

\item {} 
ignoring considerable performances differences (valuationType = ‘noVeto’).

\end{itemize}

Example:

\fvset{hllines={, ,}}%
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{xmcda}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{randomPerfTabs} \PYG{k}{import} \PYG{n}{RandomCBPerformanceTableau}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RandomCBPerformanceTableau}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{saveXMCDA2}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{example}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{xmcda}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{showXMCDARubisBestChoiceRecommendation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{problemFileName}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{example}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}


Back to the {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:technical-label}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{std,std-ref}{Installation}}}}

\index{PreRankedConfidentOutrankingDigraph (class in sparseOutrankingDigraphs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedConfidentOutrankingDigraph}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{sparseOutrankingDigraphs.}\sphinxbfcode{PreRankedConfidentOutrankingDigraph}}{\emph{argPerfTab}, \emph{quantiles=None}, \emph{quantilesOrderingStrategy='average'}, \emph{LowerClosed=False}, \emph{componentRankingRule='Copeland'}, \emph{minimalComponentSize=1}, \emph{distribution='triangular'}, \emph{betaParameter=2}, \emph{confidence=90.0}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{tempDir=None}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=1}, \emph{nbrOfThreads=1}, \emph{save2File=None}, \emph{CopyPerfTab=True}, \emph{Comments=False}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph}}}}, {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Main class for the multiprocessing implementation of pre-ranked sparse confident outranking digraphs.

The sparse outranking digraph instance is decomposed with a confident q-tiling sort into a partition
of quantile equivalence classes which are linearly ordered by average quantile limits (default).

With each quantile equivalence class is associated a ConfidentBipolarOutrankingDigraph object
which is restricted to the decision actions gathered in this quantile equivalence class.

By default, the number of quantiles is set to a tenth of the number of decision actions,
i.e. quantiles = order//10. The effective number of quantiles can be much lower for large orders;
for instance quantiles = 250 gives good results for a digraph of order 25000.

For other parameters settings, see the corresponding classes:
{\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:sortingDigraphs.QuantilesSortingDigraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{sortingDigraphs.QuantilesSortingDigraph}}}} and {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:outrankingDigraphs.ConfidentBipolarOutrankingDigraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{outrankingDigraphs.ConfidentBipolarOutrankingDigraph}}}} .
\index{computeCLTLikelihoods() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedConfidentOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedConfidentOutrankingDigraph.computeCLTLikelihoods}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeCLTLikelihoods}}{\emph{distribution='triangular'}, \emph{betaParameter=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Renders the pairwise CLT likelihood of the at least as good as relation
neglecting all considerable large performance differences polarisations.

\end{fulllineitems}

prints the relation valuation in actions X actions table format.

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PreRankedOutrankingDigraph (class in sparseOutrankingDigraphs)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{class }\sphinxcode{sparseOutrankingDigraphs.}\sphinxbfcode{PreRankedOutrankingDigraph}}{\emph{argPerfTab}, \emph{quantiles=None}, \emph{quantilesOrderingStrategy='average'}, \emph{LowerClosed=False}, \emph{componentRankingRule='Copeland'}, \emph{minimalComponentSize=1}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{tempDir=None}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=1}, \emph{nbrOfThreads=1}, \emph{save2File=None}, \emph{CopyPerfTab=True}, \emph{Comments=False}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph}}}}, {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{perfTabs.PerformanceTableau}}}}

Main class for the multiprocessing implementation of pre-ranked sparse outranking digraphs.

The sparse outranking digraph instance is decomposed with a q-tiling sort into a partition
of quantile equivalence classes which are linearly ordered by average quantile limits (default).

With each quantile equivalence class is associated a BipolarOutrankingDigraph object
which is restricted to the decision actions gathered in this quantile equivalence class.

See \sphinxurl{http://leopold-loewenheim.uni.lu/bisdorff/documents/DA2PL-RB-2016.pdf}

By default, the number of quantiles is set to a tenth of the number of decision actions,
i.e. quantiles = order//10. The effective number of quantiles can be much lower for large orders;
for instance quantiles = 250 gives good results for a digraph of order 25000.

For other parameters settings, see the corresponding {\hyperref[\detokenize{techDoc:sortingDigraphs.QuantilesSortingDigraph}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{sortingDigraphs.QuantilesSortingDigraph}}}} class.
\index{actionOrder() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph.actionOrder}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{actionOrder}}{\emph{action}, \emph{ordering=None}}{}
Renders the order of a decision action in a given ordering

If ordering == None, the self.boostedOrder attribute is used.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{actionRank() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}

Renders the rank of a decision action in a given ranking

If ranking == None, the self.boostedRanking attribute is used.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeActionCategories() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}

Renders the union of categories in which the given action is sorted positively or null into.
Returns a tuple : action, lowest category key, highest category key, membership credibility !

\end{fulllineitems}

on each component.

decreasing preference direction following the rankingRule
on each component.

\index{computeCriterion2RankingCorrelation() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph.computeCriterion2RankingCorrelation}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeCriterion2RankingCorrelation}}{\emph{criterion}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrOfCPUs=None}, \emph{Debug=False}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Renders the ordinal correlation coefficient between
the global linar ranking and the marginal criterion relation.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeMarginalVersusGlobalRankingCorrelations() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph.computeMarginalVersusGlobalRankingCorrelations}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeMarginalVersusGlobalRankingCorrelations}}{\emph{Sorted=True}, \emph{ValuedCorrelation=False}, \emph{Threading=False}, \emph{nbrCores=None}, \emph{Comments=False}}{}
Method for computing correlations between each individual criterion relation with the corresponding global ranking relation.

Returns a list of tuples (correlation,criterionKey) sorted by default in decreasing order of the correlation.

If Threading is True, a multiprocessing Pool class is used with a parallel equivalent of the built-in map function.

If nbrCores is not set, the os.cpu\_count() function is used to determine the number of available cores.

\end{fulllineitems}

Renders a bipolar-valued bi-dictionary relation
representing the degree of credibility of the
assertion that “actions x in A belongs to category c in C”,
i.e. x outranks low category limit and does not outrank
the high category limit (if LowerClosed).

\end{fulllineitems}

\fvset{hllines={, ,}}%
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{sparseOutrankingDigraphs} \PYG{k}{import} \PYG{o}{*}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RandomCBPerformanceTableau}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{numberOfActions}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{100}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{seed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{g} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PreRankedOutrankingDigraph}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{quantiles}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{g}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{*\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{} show short \PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}*}

\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsh{} Actions         : 100}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsh{} Criteria        : 7}
\PYG{g+go}{Sorting by        : 10\PYGZhy{}Tiling}

\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsh{} Components      : 19}
\PYG{g+go}{Minimal size      : 1}
\PYG{g+go}{Maximal size      : 22}
\PYG{g+go}{Median size       : 2}

\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}  Constructor run times (in sec.) \PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}}

\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZlt{}class \PYGZsq{}sparseOutrankingDigraphs.PreRankedOutrankingDigraph\PYGZsq{}\PYGZgt{} instance}

\index{computeDecompositionSummaryStatistics() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.computeDecompositionSummaryStatistics}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeDecompositionSummaryStatistics}}{}{}
Returns the summary of the distribution of the length of
the components as follows:

\fvset{hllines={, ,}}%
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{summary} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{max}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{maxLength}\PYG{p}{,}
           \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{median}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{medianLength}\PYG{p}{,}
           \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{mean}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{:}\PYG{n}{meanLength}\PYG{p}{,}
           \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{stdev}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{stdLength}\PYG{p}{,}
           \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{fillrate}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{n}{fillrate}\PYG{p}{,}
                      \PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{see} \PYG{n}{computeFillRate}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeDeterminateness() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.computeDeterminateness}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeDeterminateness}}{}{}
Computes the Kendalll distance in \% of self
with the all median valued (indeterminate) digraph.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeFillRate() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.computeFillRate}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeFillRate}}{}{}
Renders the sum of the squares (without diagonal) of the orders of the component’s subgraphs
over the square (without diagonal) of the big digraph order.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeOrderCorrelation() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.computeOrderCorrelation}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeOrderCorrelation}}{\emph{order}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Renders the ordinal correlation K of a sparse digraph instance
when compared with a given linear order (from worst to best) of its actions

K = sum\_\{x != y\} {[} min( max(-self.relation(x,y)),other.relation(x,y), max(self.relation(x,y),-other.relation(x,y)) {]}

K /= sum\_\{x!=y\} {[} min(abs(self.relation(x,y),abs(other.relation(x,y)) {]}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
Renders a dictionary with the key ‘correlation’ containing the actual bipolar correlation index and the key ‘determination’ containing the minimal determination level D of self and the other relation.

D = sum\_\{x != y\} min(abs(self.relation(x,y)),abs(other.relation(x,y)) / n(n-1)

where n is the number of actions considered.

The correlation index with a completely indeterminate relation
is by convention 0.0 at determination level 0.0 .
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}
self must be a normalized outranking digraph instance !
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{computeOrdinalCorrelation() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.computeOrdinalCorrelation}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{computeOrdinalCorrelation}}{\emph{other}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Renders the ordinal correlation K of a SpareOutrakingDigraph instance
when compared with a given compatible (same actions set) other Digraph instance.

K = sum\_\{x != y\} {[} min( max(-self.relation(x,y)),other.relation(x,y), max(self.relation(x,y),-other.relation(x,y)) {]}

K /= sum\_\{x!=y\} {[} min(abs(self.relation(x,y),abs(other.relation(x,y)) {]}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
The global outranking relation of SparesOutrankingDigraph instances is contructed on the fly
from the ordered dictionary of the components.

Renders a dictionary with a ‘correlation’ key containing the actual bipolar correlation index K and a ‘determination’ key containing the minimal determination level D of self and the other relation, where

D = sum\_\{x != y\} min(abs(self.relation(x,y)),abs(other.relation(x,y)) / n(n-1)

and where n is the number of actions considered.

The correlation index K with a completely indeterminate relation
is by convention 0.0 at determination level 0.0 .
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{exportGraphViz() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.exportGraphViz}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{exportGraphViz}}{\emph{fileName=None}, \emph{actionsSubset=None}, \emph{bestChoice=set()}, \emph{worstChoice=set()}, \emph{noSilent=True}, \emph{graphType='png'}, \emph{graphSize='7}, \emph{7'}, \emph{relation=None}}{}
export GraphViz dot file  for graph drawing filtering.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{exportSortingGraphViz() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.exportSortingGraphViz}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{exportSortingGraphViz}}{\emph{fileName=None}, \emph{actionsSubset=None}, \emph{direction='decreasing'}, \emph{noSilent=True}, \emph{graphType='pdf'}, \emph{graphSize='7}, \emph{7'}, \emph{fontSize=10}, \emph{relation=None}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
export GraphViz dot file for weak order (Hasse diagram) drawing
filtering from SortingDigraph instances.

Example:

\fvset{hllines={, ,}}%
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]

\PYG{g+gp}{... }                        \PYG{n}{Threading}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{MP}\PYG{p}{,}

\PYG{g+gp}{... }                            \PYG{n}{quantilesOrderingStrategy}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{average}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }                            \PYG{n}{componentRankingRule}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{Copeland}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}

\PYG{g+gp}{... }                           \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{}tempDir=\PYGZsq{}.\PYGZsq{},}

\PYG{g+gp}{... }                            \PYG{n}{Comments}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{False}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{Debug}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{False}\PYG{p}{)}

\index{htmlRelationMap() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.htmlRelationMap}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{htmlRelationMap}}{\emph{actionsSubset=None, tableTitle='Relation Map', relationName='r(x R y)', symbols={[}'+', '\&middot;', '\&nbsp;', '-', '\_'{]}, Colored=True, ContentCentered=True}}{}
renders the relation map in actions X actions html table format.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ordering2Preorder() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.ordering2Preorder}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{ordering2Preorder}}{\emph{ordering}}{}
Renders a preordering (a list of list) of a linar order (worst to best) of decision actions in increasing preference direction.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ranking2Preorder() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.ranking2Preorder}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{ranking2Preorder}}{\emph{ranking}}{}
Renders a preordering (a list of list) of a ranking (best to worst) of decision actions in increasing preference direction.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{recodeValuation() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.recodeValuation}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{recodeValuation}}{\emph{newMin=-1}, \emph{newMax=1}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Specialization for recoding the valuation of all the partial digraphs and the component relation.
By default the valuation domain is normalized to {[}-1;1{]}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{relation() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.relation}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{relation}}{\emph{x}, \emph{y}, \emph{Debug=False}}{}
Dynamic construction of the global outranking characteristic function \sphinxstyleemphasis{r(x S y)}.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{showDecomposition() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.showDecomposition}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{showDecomposition}}{\emph{direction='decreasing'}}{}
Prints on the console the decomposition structure of the sparse outranking digraph instance
in \sphinxstyleemphasis{decreasing} (default) or \sphinxstyleemphasis{increasing} preference direction.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{showHTMLMarginalQuantileLimits() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.showHTMLMarginalQuantileLimits}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{showHTMLMarginalQuantileLimits}}{}{}
shows the marginal quantiles limits.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{showHTMLRelationMap() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.showHTMLRelationMap}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{showHTMLRelationMap}}{\emph{actionsSubset=None, Colored=True, tableTitle='Relation Map', relationName='r(x S y)', symbols={[}'+', '\&middot;', '\&nbsp;', '\&\#150;', '\&\#151;'{]}}}{}
Launches a browser window with the colored relation map of self.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{showRelationMap() (sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph method)}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{techDoc:sparseOutrankingDigraphs.SparseOutrankingDigraph.showRelationMap}}\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{showRelationMap}}{\emph{fromIndex=None}, \emph{toIndex=None}, \emph{symbols=None}, \emph{actionsList=None}}{}
Prints on the console, in text map format, the location of
the diagonal outranking components of the sparse outranking digraph.
\begin{description}
\item[{By default, symbols = \{‘max’:’┬’,’positive’: ‘+’, ‘median’: ‘ ‘,}] \leavevmode
‘negative’: ‘-‘, ‘min’: ‘┴’\}

\end{description}

Example:

\fvset{hllines={, ,}}%
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{sparseOutrankingDigraphs} \PYG{k}{import} \PYG{o}{*}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{t} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{RandomCBPerformanceTableau}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{numberOfActions}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{50}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{seed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{bg} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PreRankedOutrankingDigraph}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{t}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{quantiles}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{n}{minimalComponentSize}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{bg}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{*\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{} show short \PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}*}

\PYG{g+go}{Sorting by        : 10\PYGZhy{}Tiling}
